evals.prompt: Use gh-attach-subissue to attach octo-org/platform#128 under octo-org/platform#42 as a real GitHub sub-issue. Show me the exact gh commands you would run.
evals.expected_output: Resolves parent and child node IDs, then uses a shell-safe `gh api graphql` `addSubIssue` mutation that inlines the resolved IDs into the query text.
expectations: Uses GitHub issue node IDs rather than raw issue numbers in the `addSubIssue` input.
Shows `gh api repos/.../issues/... --jq .node_id` or an equivalent node-ID lookup for both issues.
Shows a `gh api graphql` mutation for `addSubIssue`.
Avoids GraphQL variable declarations such as `mutation($parentId: ID!, ...)` inside the shell command.

evals.prompt: I already created acme/api#901. Make it a true child issue of acme/api#900 with gh. Do not use a plain `Parent: #900` body line unless GitHub sub-issues are unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Prioritizes the true GitHub sub-issue attach flow, clearly distinguishes it from a plain body reference, and only mentions the body-reference fallback as non-equivalent.
expectations: Prefers a real GitHub sub-issue attach instead of a body-reference shortcut.
States that `Parent: #900` is only a fallback or weaker alternative.
Does not claim the body-reference fallback is equivalent to a real sub-issue link.

evals.prompt: Show the safest shell command shape for linking child issue #17 under parent issue #3 in widgets/infra when shell expansion filters are touchy. I specifically do not want GraphQL `$variables` in the command.
evals.expected_output: Provides the safe command pattern that resolves node IDs first and inlines those resolved IDs into the `gh api graphql` mutation text without GraphQL variable declarations.
expectations: Explains or demonstrates resolving node IDs before the mutation.
Uses an inlined-ID `gh api graphql` `addSubIssue` command shape.
Avoids `mutation($parentId: ID!, $childId: ID!)` or similar GraphQL variable syntax.
Keeps the answer focused on shell-safe command construction.
